key_insight: TaCauchy brings physics-grounded tactile simulation into Isaac Sim by computing Cauchy stress and surface traction directly from FEM rather than learning an empirical proxy.
engineering_relevance: It enables faster, more accurate vision-based tactile data generation for robot manipulation and reinforcement learning across multiple sensor types with minimal integration effort.
method: UIPC-based FEM solver with hyperelastic constitutive laws
Direct Cauchy stress computation and projection to contact tractions/pressure
Automatic mesh generation plus modular sensor interface for GelSight Mini, DIGIT, and 9DTact
evidence_signal: The framework reports 33.40 FPS for a single environment, 555 FPS across 60 parallel environments, under 1 ms stress-extraction overhead, and SSIM above 0.93 in physical validation over 1.2556–4.7332 N.
watch_item: Accuracy and speed may depend on mesh quality, contact modeling assumptions, and sensor-specific calibration when transferring to new geometries or materials.
paper_title: TaCauchy: An Extensible FEM Framework for Vision-Based Tactile Simulation
